text: TypeScript strictness is treated as a first-class quality gate. `any` annotations are blocked in 100% of reviewed PRs — no exceptions have been accepted.
Error handling standards tightened noticeably after a production incident in Q3 2024. Since then, async functions without explicit error handling have been blocked consistently.
React hook naming violations are becoming more frequent as the codebase grows. The pattern has appeared across 4 PRs from 3 different authors in the last 30 days — possibly worth automating.
Debug `console.log` statements appear in nearly every PR review. The team has discussed adding an ESLint rule but hasn't done so yet.
